title: anthemis montana subsp. saxatilis
intro: Anthemis montana subsp. saxatilis, also known as Mountain Chamomile or Saxatile, is a subspecies of the Anthemis montana plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of using Anthemis montana subsp. saxatilis include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, as well as providing relief from menstrual cramps and other menstrual-related issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or oil to utilize its medicinal properties.
